title: ricinus communis var. inermis
intro: Ricinus communis var. inermis, commonly known as the wild castor bean or unarmed castor oil plant, is a plant species in the spurge family, characterized by its lack of thorns. The plant's seeds contain ricin, a toxic compound, but the oil extracted from its seeds, known as castor oil, has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of castor oil include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Castor oil can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as oil, capsules, or tincture, and is often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
